content.full_text:
FAIR p OLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Douglas W. Nelson 
Office of the County Counsel 
County of Madera 
200 West 4th Street, Fourth Floor 
Madera, California 93637 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-11-191 
Dear Mr. Nelson: 
November 21, 2011 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Our advice is based on the facts presented. 
The Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact 
when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) 
QUESTION 
Do Supervisors Frank Bigelow and David Rogers have a conflict of interest in 
participating in decisions regarding the Madera County General Plan Housing Element or the 
Fairmead Colony Area Plan when their spouses serve as trustees for school districts that have 
voted to sue the County of Madera concerning its adoption of the element of the Madera County 
General Plan? 
CONCLUSION 
Because the Act exempts government income from the definition of income the 
supervisors will not have a conflict of interest when participating in decisions regarding the 
Madera County General Plan Housing Element or the Fairmead Colony Area Plan. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 9l014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-11-191 
Page No. 2 
You are the County Counsel for Madera County and are writing on behalf of County 
Supervisors Frank Bigelow and David Rogers. The Supervisors would like to know if they have 
would have a conflict of interest in participating in decisions regarding the Madera County 
General Plan Housing Element or the Fairmead Colony Area Plan and any associated litigation. 
The spouses of both Supervisors are trustees in two different school districts around the area. 
Leslie Rogers serves as a trustee for the Chowchilla Elementary School District and 
Barbara Bigelow serves as a trustee for the Chawanakee Unified School District. Both of these 
districts are suing the County of Madera concerning its adoption of an updated housing element 
of the Madera County General Plan. The two Supervisors participated in the deliberations and 
voting which led to the adoption of the updated housing element prior to the lawsuit being filed. 
Adoption of the housing element is a legislative action required by state law. The two 
Supervisors have also participated in a public hearing concerning the Fairmead Colony Area 
Plan, and although the school districts have submitted comments on that plan no decision was 
rendered and although it may result in litigation, there is no pending lawsuit. 
The only compensation received by Leslie Rogers for her service on the Chowchilla 
Elementary School District Board of Trustees is health insurance. The position taken by 
Supervisor Rogers on the Madera County Housing Element would not affect that insurance. 
Barbara Bigelow receives a $100.00 a month stipend for service on the Chawanakee Unified 
School District Board of Trustees. The position taken by Supervisor Bigelow on the Madera 
County Housing Element would not affect that compensation. 
ANALYSIS 
Your question implicates the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions, which begin at Section 
87100 by stating 
"No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, 
participate in making. or in any way attempt to use his official position to 
influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know 
he has a financial interest." 
As members of the Madera County Board of Supervisors, Supervisors Bigelow and 
Rogers arc public officials within the meaning of Section 871002• The "financial interests'" noted 
i11 1he -;fc!ntte, 1.\'hich may ǖ!iv1.Ǘ ri'ie to a conflict of interest. ,ire (k"fin;,;J ,it Section 87 l<n(cJ lo 
include: 
"Any source of income, other than gifts ... aggregating two hundred 
fifty dollars ($500) or more in value provided to, received by or 
2 "Public officiar' means every member, officer, employee or consultant of a =tate or local government 
agency. (Section 82048.) 


File No. A-11-191 
Page No. 3 
promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when 
the decision is made." 
We assume that you have no undisclosed real property or other interests that would be 
affected by the decisions. The Political Refmm Act prohibits only financial conflicts of interest, 
and the Commission is authorized only to give advice on obligations arising under that body of 
law. 
To determine whether the school districts are "sources of income" to the Supervisors, we 
first look to the Act's definition of "income," given at Section 82030. "Income" is defined, in 
pe11inent part, as follows: 
"(a) 'Income' means, except as provided in subdivision (b), a payment 
received, including but not limited to any salary, wage, advance, dividend, 
interest, rent. proceeds from any sale, gift, including any gift of food or 
beverage, loan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness received by the filer. 
reimbursement for expenses, per diem, ... " 
This broad definition begins from a premise that, subject to exceptions stated in 
subdivision (b), any payment of money, goods, or services, is "income.'' Thus, as a general rule, 
any person or organization that has made payments of $500 or more to a public official in the 
past l 2 months is a source of income to that official for purposes of Section 87103( c). Income 
includes any community property interest in the income of a spouse. (Section 82030). 
The income you have asked about is in the form of health benefits received by Leslie 
Rogers and a $100 a month stipend received by Barbara Bigelow. 
If Supervisor Bigelow or Supervisor Rogers' community property interest in the income 
earned by their spouses from the school districts in the past twelve months, amount to $500 or 
more, they would have a financial interest in that school district as a "source of income," unless 
an exception applies. Section 82030 (b) lists exceptions to the Act's definition of "income," 
including one that applies to the circumstances you describe. Under Section 82030(b)(2), 
"income" docs not include: 
"(2) Salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem, and social 
security, disability, or other similar benefit payments received from a 
state, local, or federal government agency and reimbursement for 
trav:ǘl expenses and per diem received from a bona fide nonprofit 
entity Ǚxempt from taxation under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal 
Revenue Code." 
This provision, known as the "government salary exception," provides that even if the 
Supervisors were the beneficiaries of income or benefits from a school district due the 
community prope11y interest in the income of their spouses. those payments would not be 
--------------


File No. A-11-191 
Page No. 4 
considered "income" within the meaning of the Act. The school districts would not be a "source 
of income'' to the Supervisors within the meaning of Section 87103 because the school districts 
are local government agencies. These payments cannot, therefore, give rise to a conflict of 
interest as that term is used in the Act. 
A conflict of interest may also exist if a particular decision affects the Supervisors' 
personal finances beyond their interest in their spouses stipend income and/or health benefits. 
(MerrfrtmAdvice Letter, No. I-00-142.) However, you stated this was not the case thus the 
official would not need to disqualify him or herself from the decisions in question. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
SKB:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
Sukhi K. Brar 
Counsel, Legal Division

General Plan?

Because the Act exempts government income from the definition of income the 
supervisors will not have a conflict of interest when participating in decisions regarding the 
Madera County General Plan Housing Element or the Fairmead Colony Area Plan.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do Supervisors Frank Bigelow and David Rogers have a conflict of interest in 
participating in decisions regarding the Madera County General Plan Housing Element or the 
Fairmead Colony Area Plan when their spouses serve as trustees for school districts that have 
voted to sue the County of Madera concerning its adoption of the element of the Madera County 
General Plan?

CONCLUSION: Because the Act exempts government income from the definition of income the 
supervisors will not have a conflict of interest when participating in decisions regarding the 
Madera County General Plan Housing Element or the Fairmead Colony Area Plan.
